title: Visual Reinforcement Learning
learningObjectives: Explain why pixel-based observations make RL harder than state-based RL, and how convolutional encoders extract useful features from raw frames.
Build a preprocessing pipeline (grayscale, resize, normalise) and describe why each step is standard practice in visual RL.
Train a PPO agent with CnnPolicy on a visual control task and interpret the learning curve relative to a random-policy baseline.
Explain what frame stacking solves (partial observability / motion perception) and implement it with VecFrameStack.
Compare convergence speed and final performance across single-frame vs stacked inputs on the same environment.
Identify which ViZDoom scenarios require temporal reasoning vs spatial navigation and justify algorithm or hyperparameter choices accordingly.
